DECLARATION OF ERIC MARLIN IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS' MOTION TO (1) STAY THE ACTION WHILE THIS MOTION IS PENDING (2) COMPEL ARBITRATION AND (3) DISMISS THE ACTION OR, INT HE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT MACY'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC. MONROE, MIKE MACY'S WEST, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MACY'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC., MIKE MONROE AND MACY'S WEST'S MOTION (1) STAY THE ACTION WHILE THIS MOTION IS PENDING, (2) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND (3) DISMISS THE ACTION, OR, THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS GRANTED, THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS APPRISED OF HIS RIGHT TO OPT OUT OF THE ARBITRATION PROGRAM AND ON TWO OCCASIONS AND DID NOT DO SO. THE PROGRAM IS NEITHER PROCEDURALLY NOR SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED BUT FOR THE PHRASE "IN PRACTICE" IN PARAGRAPH FIFTEEN OF THE GLICK DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Defendant Macy'S Department Stores, Inc., Mike Monroe, Macy'S West, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion To 1)Stay The Action While This Motion Is Pending; 2)Compel Arbitration And 3) Dismiss The Action Or, The Alternatve, Stay The Action Pending Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice; Appendix Of Out Of State Authorities.
Off Calendar Feb-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MACY'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC., MIKE MONROE AND MACY'S WEST'S MOTION (1) STAY THE ACTION WHILE THIS MOTION IS PENDING, (2) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND (3) DISMISS THE ACTION, OR, THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE.
APPEARANCES: SONYA SMALLETS, ESQUIRE AND VALERIE MCGINTY, ESQUIRE (LAWLESS & LAWLESS, 415.391.7555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ANDREW M. WOLFE, ESQUIRE (ROPERS MAJESKI KOHN BENTLEY, 415.972.6352) AND BETTY THORNE TIERNEY, SENIOR COUNSEL (MACY'S LAW DEPARTMENT, 314. 342.6728) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS GRANTED, THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS APPRISED OF HIS RIGHT TO OPT OUT OF THE ARBITRATION PROGRAM AND ON TWO OCCASIONS AND DID NOT DO SO. THE PROGRAM IS NEITHER PROCEDURALLY NOR SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED BUT FOR THE PHRASE "IN PRACTICE" IN PARAGRAPH FIFTEEN OF THE GLICK DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
